results.title: Europe Has Economic Power. Can It Use It Against Trump’s Tariffs?
results.abstract: European leaders have said they would prefer to negotiate. If that fails, their response could go beyond anything they’ve tried before.

multimedia.caption: Ursula von der Leyen, president of the E.U. executive arm, said Europeans felt “let down by our oldest ally.”
Ursula von der Leyen, president of the E.U. executive arm, said Europeans felt “let down by our oldest ally.”

results.title: Trump’s Tariffs Don’t Apply to Chips, but Taiwan Remains Wary
results.abstract: The chip companies in Taiwan, the center of the global supply chain, are expected to face pressure from Washington to invest more in the U.S.

multimedia.caption: Advanced chips like the ones made at Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company are some of Taiwan’s main exports.

results.title: Bruce Springsteen Will Release Seven ‘Lost Albums’ in June
results.abstract: The singer and songwriter announced a boxed set featuring 83 songs, of which 74 have never been officially released in any form.

multimedia.caption: “‘The Lost Albums’ were full records, some of them even to the point of being mixed and not released,” Bruce Springsteen said in a statement.

results.title: Trump Took a Wrecking Ball to Southeast Asia’s Role as an Alternative to China
results.abstract: Punishing tariffs on Vietnam, Cambodia, Thailand and others in the region threaten their position as major manufacturing hubs for the American market.

multimedia.caption: A garment factory in Vietnam. The Trump administration slapped steep tariffs on the country that could have lasting effects on global trade.

results.title: Israel Hits Syria With New Strikes and Ground Raid as Tensions Soar
results.abstract: Israel defended the moves as necessary security measures, but Syria accused it of trying to destabilize the country.

multimedia.caption: The site of an Israeli airstrike in the southern Hama governorate in Syria on Thursday.
The site of an Israeli airstrike in the southern Hama governorate in Syria on Thursday.

results.title: How Countries Responded to Trump’s Tariffs
results.abstract: China vowed countermeasures and the E.U. promised a unified response, while Britain and Japan refrained from immediate retaliation.

multimedia.caption: Britain’s Prime Minister Keir Starmer said that negotiations with the United States toward a trade deal would continue.
Britain’s Prime Minister Keir Starmer said that negotiations with the United States toward a trade deal would continue.

results.title: The Fallout From Trump’s Reign of Tariffs
results.abstract: Global markets were in a tailspin on Thursday as policymakers and trade partners expressed dismay over president’s latest trade-war salvo.
results.byline: By Andrew Ross Sorkin, Ravi Mattu, Bernhard Warner, Sarah Kessler, Michael J. de la Merced, Lauren Hirsch and Edmund Lee

multimedia.caption: President Trump sees his latest tariff barrage ushering in a “golden age.” Investors and U.S. allies view the action differently.

results.title: Why Did Trump Spare Russia From New Tariffs?
results.abstract: The Trump administration says sanctions imposed on Moscow mean the U.S. does little trade with Russia, but questions persist about the motivations.

multimedia.caption: Skyscrapers in Moscow. Trade between the two countries has dropped, but Russia still exported about $3 billion worth of goods to the United States last year.

results.title: What Is Your Reaction to the Arrests of Student Protesters by Federal Immigration Agents?
results.abstract: The Trump administration has targeted at least nine foreign pro-Palestinian students and academics for deportation. Do you support these efforts, or do you think they are a violation of civil liberties?

results.title: Kremlin Envoy Says He’s in U.S. for Talks With Trump Administration
results.abstract: Kirill Dmitriev wrote on social media that his meetings were about restoring a U.S.-Russian dialogue that was “completely destroyed under the Biden administration.”

multimedia.caption: Kirill Dmitriev, the head of Russia’s sovereign wealth fund and President Vladimir V. Putin’s special representative for investment and economic cooperation, in Saudi Arabia in February.

results.title: Insulin Test Used to Convict Lucy Letby in Babies’ Deaths Was Unreliable, Experts Say
results.abstract: A new report by neonatal and toxicology experts casts doubt on the test results that were pivotal to the case against the British nurse.

multimedia.caption: Mark McDonald, Ms. Letby’s lawyer, presented new expert reports on Thursday to Britain’s Criminal Cases Review Commission, an independent body that investigates possible miscarriages of justice.

results.title: We Are in Uncharted Waters With Trump’s New Tariffs
results.abstract: Trump had a point that pundits had been wrong about NAFTA and wrong about China. But where he loses the plot is in the chaos surrounding his tariff announcements.

results.title: Pressing Assault, Israel Shifts the Goal Posts in Gaza
results.abstract: Israel wants to squeeze Hamas into releasing the dozens of hostages still held in the enclave. But other objectives have since emerged.

results.title: Eric Adams to Run as an Independent, Skipping Democratic Primary
results.abstract: Mayor Eric Adams faced an uphill battle in the June 24 Democratic primary, which has drawn a crowded field of candidates. He will run for re-election as an independent instead.
